content: I'll start by reading the role documentation and current project state.

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, the refactor agent, or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) — use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)
19	- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` (symlinked from Archon) — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini) for informal mathematical reasoning
20	
21	## Key Files & Permissions
22	
23	All state files are in `.archon/`:
24	
25	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Refactor Agent | Review Agent | User |
26	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
27	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | read only | read only | read |
28	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
29	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
30	| `.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` | **write** | do not read | **read** (then execute) | do not read | read |
31	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | read only | read only | read |
32	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | read only | read only | read |
33	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (refactor.md) | read only | read |
34	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
35	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
36	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read + limited write** (file path rename only) | read | **write** |
37	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | **write (all files; protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed)** | do not edit | write (via comments) |
38	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
39	
40	## Protected Declarations
41	
42	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
43	
44	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
45	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
46	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed. 
47	
48	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
49	
50	The blueprint uses two active markers.
51	
52	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
53	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
54	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
55	
56	**The review agent is the sole writer of these markers.** Prover agents (autoformalize / prover / polish) never touch the blueprint chapters — they describe their outcome in `task_results/<file>.md` and the review agent verifies and marks accordingly. The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints, but never markers.
57	
58	## User Interaction
59	
60	Users provide hints in two places:
61	
62	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
63	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
64	
65	## Agent Roles
66	
67	### Plan Agent
68	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
69	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
70	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
71	- Read `.archon/task_results/refactor.md` - if refactor agent has run, read their report and adjust your plans accordingly
72	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
73	- Write `.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` when structural changes are needed
74	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
75	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
76	
77	### Prover Agent
78	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
79	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
80	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
81	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
82	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
83	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
84	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
85	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
86	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
87	
88	### Refactor Agent
89	- Read `.archon/prompts/refactor.md` for your full instructions
90	- Read `.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` for the plan agent's directive
91	- Modify any `.lean` file: definitions, signatures, types, imports
92	- Keep all files compiling (insert `sorry` at broken proof sites)
93	- Do NOT fill proofs — that's the prover's job
94	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/refactor.md`
95	
96	### Review Agent
97	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
98	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
99	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
100	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
101	- **Update blueprint markers** (`\leanok`, `\mathlibok`) in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` based on verified compilation and the provers' task results
102	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md

1	# Project Progress

3	## Current Stage
4	prover  (iteration 015 — Path-2 `H⁰` algebraic bridge for `HModule'`, refactor-then-prover phase. The iter-014 prover round closed `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule'` cleanly: sorry count 10 → 9, kernel-only axioms, body adopted as the probe-confirmed `Abelian.Ext ((presheafToSheaf _ _).obj ((yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj X)) F n` (qualified `Abelian.Ext` spelling for stylistic consistency with iter-009/010). Iter-015 plan-agent has scoped + probe-confirmed the next single-declaration sub-step: `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule'_zero_linearEquiv` — the `H⁰` algebraic bridge for `HModule'`, mirroring iter-010's `HModule_zero_linearEquiv` for the iter-014 carrier. Closure body: probe-confirmed one-liner `Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀`. Iter-015 sorry count `9 → 10 → 9` over the iteration. Refactor agent runs first to scaffold; prover agent then closes.)

6	## Stages
7	- [x] init
8	- [x] autoformalize
9	- [ ] prover
10	- [ ] polish

12	## Big-picture context
13	
14	This project formalizes the Jacobian of a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over a field, following Christian Merten's challenge (`references/challenge.lean`). The nine declarations in `archon-protected.yaml` are the deliverables — their signatures are frozen.
15	
16	`STRATEGY.md` describes the multi-phase build-out path (Phases A–E). Iter-005/006/007 closed Phase A steps 1–5 + polish. Iter-008 closed Track 2 (étale sheafification of `PicardFunctorAb`). Iter-009 closed the Phase A step 5/6 bridge (`HModule` infrastructure for `ModuleCat k`-valued sheaf cohomology). Iter-010 closed the Phase A step 6 *algebraic preliminary* (`HModule_zero_linearEquiv`). Iter-011 closed the **first protected sorry** (`AlgebraicGeometry.genus`). Iter-012 opened Path-2 Čech scaffold (`Scheme.cechCochain_OC`, `Scheme.cechCohomology_OC`), both honest one-liner closures. Iter-014 closed the Mayer-Vietoris prerequisite `Scheme.HModule'` (cohomology of an open in `ModuleCat k` flavor). Iter-015 opens the natural follow-on companion: `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule'_zero_linearEquiv` — the `H⁰` algebraic bridge for `HModule'`, completing the algebraic-side preliminaries for the Mayer-Vietoris LES (queued iter-016+) and the Stein-factorization-derived `H⁰(C, O_C) ≃ k` argument (queued iter-017+).
17	
18	## Iteration 015 plan-agent first pass (this iteration)
19	
20	Plan-agent verification of the iter-014 prover round:
21	
22	- **Iter-014 prover outcome — sorry count**: `${LEAN4_PYTHON_BIN:-python3} "$LEAN4_SCRIPTS/sorry_analyzer.py" AlgebraicJacobian/ --format=summary` returns `9 total across 3 file(s)`. Distribution: 5 `Jacobian.lean` + 3 `AbelJacobi.lean` + 1 `Picard/Functor.lean` (the file `Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean` drops off the list entirely). Pre-prover `10`, post-prover `9`. Matches expected `10 → 9` exactly.
23	- **Iter-014 prover outcome — file diagnostics**: `lean_diagnostic_messages AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean` returns `{success: true, items: [], failed_dependencies: []}`. Zero warnings, zero errors.
24	- **Iter-014 prover outcome — axioms**: `lean_verify` on `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule'` returns `{axioms: [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound], warnings: []}`. Kernel-only.
25	- **Iter-014 prover outcome — body adopted**: the prover used the qualified `Abelian.Ext` spelling (rather than the unqualified `Ext`) for stylistic consistency with iter-009 `HModule` and iter-010 `HModule_zero_linearEquiv`. The directive explicitly allowed either spelling; both are mathematically equivalent.
26	- **Iter-014 prover outcome — smoke test**: `Module.finrank k (Scheme.HModule' k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1 X) : ℕ` typechecks via `lean_run_code` against the post-prover file with `{success: true, diagnostics: []}`.
27	- **`Genus.lean` preserved**: still `{success: true, items: [], failed_dependencies: []}`; iter-011 first-protected closure intact.
28	- **`archon-protected.yaml`**: `git diff archon-protected.yaml` returns empty. Unchanged.
29	- **No new axioms**: `grep -rn "^axiom" AlgebraicJacobian/` returns empty.
30	
31	The iter-014 prover round is therefore **accepted**. Iter-014 closure migrated to `task_done.md`; refactor history entry recorded in `task_pending.md`.
32	
33	Plan-agent re-probe (this pass) of the iter-015 candidate body:
34	
35	- **Probe 1** (`lean_run_code`, post-iter-014 file): `noncomputable def HModule'_zero_linearEquiv (k : Type u) [Field k] {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C} [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)] [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))] (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (X : C) : HModule' k F 0 X ≃ₗ[k] ((presheafToSheaf _ _).obj ((yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj X) ⟶ F) := Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀` returns `{success: true, diagnostics: []}`.
36	- **Probe 2** (`lean_run_code`, smoke test): a concrete `noncomputable example` on `Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` for `(C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) (X : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)` consuming the new declaration returns `{success: true, diagnostics: []}`. The probe verifies that the iter-014 `HModule'` reducibility surfaces the required `Module k` and `AddCommGroup` instances on the LHS through the `Linear k` enrichment chain.
37	
38	Iter-015 work scope is therefore **scaffold + close `Scheme.HModule'_zero_linearEquiv` in `Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean`**. See `REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` for the refactor agent's exact instructions; the prover round closes with the probe-confirmed one-liner `Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀`.

40	## Iteration 015 phase
41	
42	This iteration runs in two sub-phases:
43	
44	1. **Refactor sub-phase (first).** The refactor agent reads `.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` and appends a single new declaration `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule'_zero_linearEquiv` to `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean` with body `:= sorry`. Sorry count `9 → 10`. Plan-agent (post-refactor pass) verifies and confirms the body is drop-in.
45	2. **Prover sub-phase (second, this `## Current Objectives` describes it).** The prover agent fills the body with the probe-confirmed one-liner. Sorry count `10 → 9`.
46	
47	The `## Current Objectives` below names the file the **prover** agent will work on after the refactor sub-phase completes. In this iteration's first pass (the current one) the plan agent has only written the refactor directive and this PROGRESS.md; the dispatcher will run the refactor agent next, then re-invoke the plan agent for post-refactor verification, then run the prover agent on this objective.
48	
49	## Current Objectives
50	
51	Single-file objective for the iter-015 prover round (post-refactor). The refactor sub-phase scaffolds the new declaration in unprotected territory; the prover sub-phase closes it with the probe-confirmed term-mode one-liner.
52	
53	### 1. **`AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean`** — close the `HModule'_zero_linearEquiv` body.
54	
55	**Blueprint reference:** `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_StructureSheafModuleK.tex`, the section "The $H^0$ algebraic bridge for $\HModulePrime$ (iter-015)" containing `def:Scheme_HModule_prime_zero_linearEquiv` (already written by the iter-015 plan-agent first pass, this iteration).
56	
57	#### Goal
58	
59	Close one `:= sorry` body with a probe-confirmed term-mode one-liner. The new declaration `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule'_zero_linearEquiv` will be inserted by the iter-015 refactor agent inside `namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme`, immediately after `Scheme.HModule'` (current L232–L238) and before the closing `end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme` (current L240).
60	
61	The signature (per the iter-015 refactor directive):
62	
63	```lean
64	noncomputable def HModule'_zero_linearEquiv
65	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
66	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
67	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
68	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
69	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (X : C) :
70	    HModule' k F 0 X ≃ₗ[k]
71	      ((presheafToSheaf _ _).obj
72	        ((yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj X) ⟶ F) := sorry
73	```
74	
75	#### Probe-confirmed closure body
76	
77	Replace the `sorry` body with the term-mode one-liner below. **The body has been probe-confirmed (`lean_run_code`) by the iter-015 plan-agent against the post-iter-014 file with `{success: true, diagnostics: []}`** — drop-in, no adjustment expected. The refactor agent will additionally re-run the same probe against the post-refactor file as a sanity check (per directive §5).
78	
79	```lean
80	-- Body for HModule'_zero_linearEquiv (replaces `sorry`)
81	Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀
82	```
83	
84	Notes on name resolution (relevant if Lean elaboration surfaces a unification ambiguity that the live probe missed):
85	
86	- The file has `open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry` at L38. Inside `namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme` (the new declaration's namespace), the bare name `Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀` resolves to `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀` directly — no prefix adjustment needed.
87	- The optional explicit `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀` form is also allowed; both spellings are mathematically equivalent.
88	- An equivalent body is `Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀ (R := k)` — explicitly pins the ring (only useful if elaboration ever fails to infer `R = k` from the codomain `≃ₗ[k]`, which the probes confirm does not happen).
89	- Do **not** rename the declaration, change the codomain, change the namespace, change the `noncomputable def` modifier to `noncomputable abbrev` (over-exposes the body without benefit; `def` is correct mirroring iter-010), or alter any prior declaration in the file.
90	
91	#### Verification the prover should run before reporting
92	
93	1. `lean_diagnostic_messages /home/archon/Lean_tests/AlgebraicJacobian/AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean` should return `{success: true, items: [], failed_dependencies: []}` (no warnings, no errors).
94	2. `lean_verify` on `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule'_zero_linearEquiv` should return `{axioms: [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound], warnings: []}`.
95	3. `${LEAN4_PYTHON_BIN:-python3} "$LEAN4_SCRIPTS/sorry_analyzer.py" AlgebraicJacobian/ --format=summary` should return `9 total across 3 file(s)` (5 `Jacobian.lean` + 3 `AbelJacobi.lean` + 1 `Picard/Functor.lean`; the file `Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean` should drop off the list).
96	4. **Smoke test**: a concrete `HModule'_zero_linearEquiv k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) X` for `C : Over (Spec (.of k))` and `X : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat` should typecheck against the post-prover file (probe-confirmed iter-015 plan-agent). The prover does not need to add this as a project file declaration; verifying it via `lean_multi_attempt` or `lean_run_code` after the closure suffices.
97	
98	#### Forbidden shortcuts (iter-015 specific)
99	
100	- Do **not** close with `≃` (`Abelian.Ext.homEquiv₀`) or `≃+` (`Abelian.Ext.addEquiv₀`) instead of `≃ₗ[k]` (`Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀`) — the `k`-linear refinement is the chosen one (downstream `Module.Finite k`-transport via `Module.Finite.equiv` requires it; resolved iter-010 for the parallel `HModule_zero_linearEquiv`).
101	- Do **not** close with vacuous types (`PUnit`, `LinearEquiv.refl _ PUnit`, etc.) — the body must be the genuine `Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀` application.
102	- Do **not** scaffold a new file — placement inside `Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean` is intentional and minimal-surface; future split (`HModule.lean`, `Cech.lean`) is iter-016+ work when the file outgrows ~400 LOC (currently ~285 LOC after iter-015).
103	- Do **not** introduce new `axiom` declarations or new typeclass requirements beyond what the signature lists (`[HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)] [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]` — all auto-inferable for the `Opens.grothendieckTopology` topology).
104	- Do **not** touch any file other than `Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean`. In particular: `Genus.lean` (iter-011 closure intact), `Jacobian.lean`, `AbelJacobi.lean`, `Picard/Functor.lean`, `Picard/FunctorAb.lean`, `Cohomology/StructureSheafAb.lean`, `Cohomology/SheafCompose.lean`, `Picard/LineBundle.lean`, `Rigidity.lean` — all out of scope.
105	- Do **not** alter the iter-005/006/007/009/010/012/014 closures of any prior declaration in this file. In particular: do not modify `HModule` (L185), `HModule_zero_linearEquiv` (L203), `HModule'` (L232), `cechCochain_OC` (post-refactor L~268), or `cechCohomology_OC` (post-refactor L~281).
106	- Do **not** scaffold a companion `cohomologyPresheaf` / `cohomologyFunctor` declaration in iter-015. Those are queued for iter-016+ as small functorial wrappers (~5 LOC each, single-iteration).
107	- Do **not** change `def` to `abbrev` — over-exposes the body without benefit.
108	
109	## Off-limits this iteration
110	
111	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus.lean` — closed iter-011; do not edit.
112	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean` (5 protected sorries) — gated on Phase C representability + Albanese, multi-iteration. The user's `noncomputable` authorisation extends here but is not actionable yet.
113	- `AlgebraicJacobian/AbelJacobi.lean` (3 protected sorries) — gated on `Jacobian C` first, multi-iteration.
114	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/Functor.lean` — `PicardFunctor.representable` (L185) is documented dead-end deferral until `MonoidalCategory X.Modules` lands in Mathlib + the FGA argument is built.
115	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/SheafCompose.lean`, `Cohomology/StructureSheafAb.lean`, `Picard/LineBundle.lean`, `Picard/FunctorAb.lean`, `Rigidity.lean` — closed in earlier iterations; nothing to do this round.
116	
117	## Known dead ends (do not retry)
118	
119	- The 8 currently-blocked protected sorries (`Jacobian.lean` × 5, `AbelJacobi.lean` × 3) — gated on Phase C representability + Phase E geometric content + multi-iteration upstream Mathlib advances. Do not assign as direct prover objectives. The user's `noncomputable` authorisation removes the modifier-level gate but the upstream content gates remain.
120	- `PicardFunctor.representable` (`Picard/Functor.lean` L185) — closing on the global-sections-approximate `LineBundle` is a forbidden shortcut. Honest closure requires `LineBundle` refinement (gated on `MonoidalCategory X.Modules`, still absent in current Mathlib) plus the FGA argument. Multi-iteration; do not retry.
121	- `LineBundle` direct refinement — gated on `MonoidalCategory X.Modules` (still absent; iter-015 re-probe). Multi-iteration; do not issue as a primary objective.
122	- Vacuous-functor closures (`Discrete PUnit`, etc.) — destroy the downstream Picard chain; rejected on sight.
123	- Vacuous-sheaf closures (`PUnit.{u+1}`-valued, etc.) — destroy the downstream cohomology chain; rejected on sight.
124	- For iter-012 `cechCochain_OC` / `cechCohomology_OC` (now closed): vacuous-type closures (`PUnit`, `ULift PUnit`, `ModuleCat.of k PUnit`) — rejected on sight.
125	- For iter-014 `HModule'` (now closed): closing with vacuous types or with `def` rather than `noncomputable abbrev` — rejected on sight (probe-verified the `def` form fails instance synthesis, same as iter-009 `HModule`).
126	- For iter-015 `HModule'_zero_linearEquiv`: closing with `≃` or `≃+` instead of `≃ₗ[k]`, or closing with `abbrev` rather than `def` — rejected on sight (the `k`-linear refinement is the chosen one; `def` mirrors iter-010's design).
127	- New `axiom` declarations — forbidden by plan-agent rules.
128	- Symmetric topological-equality form of rigidity (`∀ x ∈ U, g₁.left.base x = g₂.left.base x ⇒ g₁ = g₂`) — false in characteristic `p` (Frobenius). Resolved iter-002.
129	- Naive `AddCommGrpCat.uliftFunctor` (without explicit universe annotations) — does not unify under `u+1 =?= max u v`; explicit `.{u+1, u}` annotation required (resolved iter-008).
130	- `HModule` as `def` instead of `noncomputable abbrev` — instance synthesis fails on the wrapper (probe-verified iter-009; same applies to iter-014 `HModule'`).
131	- `HModule_zero_linearEquiv` with `≃` (`homEquiv₀`) or `≃+` (`addEquiv₀`) instead of `≃ₗ[k]` (`linearEquiv₀`) — the chosen signature is `≃ₗ[k]`; downstream `Module.Finite k` transport requires the `k`-linear refinement (resolved iter-010; same rationale applies to iter-015).
132	- Polish-shape `HModule_forget` (`forget₂ HModule ↔ Sheaf.H ((sheafCompose _ (forget₂)).obj F)`) — Mathlib's `Sheaf.H` uses `AddCommGrpCat.of (ULift ℤ)` as the unit, whereas `HModule` uses `ModuleCat.of k k`; the comparison is not a one-liner (iter-010 plan-agent finding).
133	- Track 1 / Phase A step 6 *proper* (Serre finiteness on a proper curve) — multi-iteration; iter-015 lays only the `H⁰` algebraic bridge for the iter-014 `HModule'` carrier, not the Mayer-Vietoris LES (queued iter-016+), the comparison theorem (queued iter-017+), or the affine finiteness/dimension-vanishing arguments (queued iter-018+/iter-019+).
134	- `Sheaf.val`-style spellings (deprecated in current Mathlib) — use `(sheafToPresheaf _ _).obj F` / `(presheafToSheaf _ _).obj` instead.
135	- Bare `Opens C.left.toTopCat` *inside* `def Scheme.foo` (auto-opens `Scheme` sub-namespace; resolves to `Scheme.Opens` expecting a `Scheme`) — use `TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat` instead. The iter-015 `HModule'_zero_linearEquiv` has no explicit `Opens` arg in its signature (the `(X : C)` is generic), so this does not apply directly.

1	# Plan Agent
2	
3	You are the plan agent. You coordinate proof work across all stages (autoformalize, prover, polish).
4	
5	## Iteration number — canonical
6	
7	Your invocation prompt always contains a line `Archon iteration: NNN`. That is the canonical iteration counter — it is what Archon writes to `logs/iter-NNN/` and stamps into commit messages (`archon[NNN/phase]`). Use it verbatim in every iteration-aware artefact you write: `STRATEGY.md` (current-state header, Revision log entries), `PROGRESS.md`, `REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`, blueprint chapter notes, etc. Do not invent your own counter and do not trust the counter you find in older artefacts (the proof journal, prior `STRATEGY.md` entries, prior `recommendations.md` files): earlier agents drifted, and you align everything new to the number in your prompt header. When you next rewrite a section that contains a wrong iteration number, fix the number in place.
8	
9	The session counter under `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` is independent — it counts prover rounds only and is not an iteration number.
10	
11	## Your Job
12	
13	1. Read `USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate user hints into your planning, then clear the file after acting
14	2. Read `task_results/` — collect prover results from each `<file>.md`, then merge findings into `task_pending.md` (update attempts) and `task_done.md` (migrate resolved theorems). Clear processed result files. If the refactor agent has run, read `task_results/refactor.md` and adjust your plans accordingly (see "Post-Refactor Verification" below).
15	3. Read `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md` to recover context — do not repeat documented dead ends
16	4. Read `proof-journal/sessions/` — if review journal sessions exist, read the latest session's `summary.md` and `recommendations.md` for the review agent's analysis. Also read `PROJECT_STATUS.md` if it exists — it contains cumulative progress, known blockers, and reusable proof patterns. Use these findings when setting objectives.
17	5. **Read and revise `STRATEGY.md`** — see "Long-arc Strategy" below. Always do this *before* writing prover objectives or a refactor directive: it forces you to think about how this iteration fits into the path to project completion, instead of optimizing locally.
18	6. Evaluate each task: is it completed, can it be completed, and if not why not? Should a refactor be triggered?
19	7. Verify prover reports independently (check sorry count and compilation) — do not trust self-reports
20	8. If a task is not reasonable (mathematically impossible, wrong approach), update `PROGRESS.md` with a corrected plan
21	9. **Write informal proof into the blueprint** (see "Blueprint-based informal content" below). Ensure that the blueprint files are always consistent with the current state of the project. You should always ensure that it is aligned with the current state of the project. 
22	10. Set clear, self-contained objectives for the next prover iteration
23	11. Do NOT write formal proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself. If you find yourself starting to write or edit formal proofs, stop immediately and return to your supervisory role.
24	12. Detect critical issues in the project (such as wrong definitions, false statements, flawed proof strategies, axioms, etc.) and address them, even if they have been present since the beginning.
25	
26	**Write permissions**: You may write to `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, `USER_HINTS.md` (to clear it), `REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` (to request structural changes), `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (to write/update informal proof), and `blueprint/src/macros/common.tex` (to add macro definitions for any non-standard LaTeX commands you use). You must NOT edit `.lean` files or `task_results/` files.
27	
28	**LaTeX macros — define before you use.** The dashboard renders blueprint chapters with KaTeX, which only knows the standard LaTeX/AMS macros (full list: https://katex.org/docs/supported.html). If you write a non-standard command in a chapter — `\Spec`, `\Pic`, `\Jac`, `\Aut`, `\Hom`, custom operators, `\mathfrak`-named shortcuts, etc. — define it in `blueprint/src/macros/common.tex` *before* using it, e.g. `\DeclareMathOperator{\Spec}{Spec}` or `\newcommand{\Jac}[1]{\mathrm{Jac}(#1)}`. Otherwise the command renders as a broken token in the dashboard. Definitions in `common.tex` are picked up by both the PDF build and the dashboard.
29	
30	**`## Current Objectives` is for files the prover should work on — nothing else.** The dispatcher fans out one prover per `.lean` file referenced in that section's headings/bullets. If you mention an off-limits file (e.g. "### 3. The protected files (`Genus.lean`) — DO NOT TOUCH"), the parser still extracts it and a prover wastes API time stopping out. Two rules:
31	- Do not list off-limits / DO-NOT-TOUCH / "skip this" files in `## Current Objectives`. Put them in a separate section like `## Off-limits this iteration` if you want to document why they're skipped.
32	- The objectives section should contain exactly the files you want a prover to attack this round, one per heading or bullet.
33	
34	**Important**: You should **NEVER** propose adding new axioms. If axioms are already present, you should remove them.
35	
36	## Protected declarations
37	
38	Read `archon-protected.yaml` at the project root. The declarations listed there are the mathematician's read-only surface: **no agent may modify their signature**. As plan agent:
39	
40	- Do not assign an objective that would require changing a protected signature.
41	- Moving a protected declaration to a different file is allowed (the refactor agent will update the YAML path), but renaming or re-signing is not.
42	
43	## References
44	
45	A paragraph-by-paragraph summary of every informal source is pasted into your prompt from `references/summary.md`. Read it every iteration. Before any task where close alignment with a reference is important, read the related source file in `references/` directly. Do not rely on memory or summaries alone. 
46	
47	If relevant, you can use Web Search to find new references, in this case you should update `references/summary.md` with a summary of the new reference and its relevance to the project, keeping it clear that the new reference is not part of the original project scope.
48	
49	## Blueprint-based informal content
50	
51	This project uses a blueprint (plasTeX + `leanblueprint`). Informal proof sketches live in `blueprint/src/chapters/<slug>.tex`, one file per Lean source file. The slug mapping is:
52	
53	```
54	Lean file  Algebra/WLocal.lean  →  chapter  blueprint/src/chapters/Algebra_WLocal.tex
55	Lean file  Core.lean            →  chapter  blueprint/src/chapters/Core.tex
56	```
57	
58	`blueprint/src/content.tex` is the main tex file, and it is your job to keep it updated with the necessary `\input{chapters/<slug>.tex}`. Your job is also to ensure that the blueprint are correct and aligned with the intended content of the project, which includes aligning the blueprint with the Lean files as they evolve. The blueprints should not be sketchy but should instead contain rigorous proofs. 
59	
60	**Before assigning a prover, ensure the relevant chapter file exists and contains the informal content the prover needs.** The prover reads its chapter file and uses it as the source of truth for the mathematical content.
61	
62	- You can create/modify/rename/delete blueprint chapters as needed, **as long as** you keep `blueprint/src/content.tex` updated and add the corresponding objective in `PROGRESS.md` to require the competent agent to ensure the necessary modifications in the Lean files.
63	- The blueprints are considered by the other agents as the source of truth for informal content, so they should always be consistent with the current state of the project; any mistake or inconsistency should be fixed as soon as possible.
64	
65	### What to write in a chapter file
66	
67	For each declaration the prover will need to handle, the chapter should contain a block like this:
68	
69	```latex
70	\begin{theorem}[name_for_humans]
71	  \label{thm:some_label}
72	  \lean{namespace.theorem_name}
73	  \uses{def:related_definition, lem:supporting_lemma}
74	  Informal statement of the theorem, using standard mathematical notation.
75	\end{theorem}
76	
77	\begin{proof}
78	  \uses{thm:another_result}
79	  Step-by-step informal proof sketch. Reference blueprint labels with \uses{...}
80	  so the dependency graph stays accurate. Use as much detail as the prover would
81	  need to formalize — a one-liner is rarely enough.
82	\end{proof}
83	```
84	
85	**Macros the prover relies on:**
86	
87	- `\lean{foo.bar}` — declares which Lean name this block corresponds to
88	- `\leanok` — added by the prover once formalization is complete (you do not add it)
89	- `\mathlibok` — added when the declaration already exists in Mathlib. Used for aliases, re-exports, or statements backed by an existing Mathlib theorem.
90	
91	### Record where the informal content lives
92	
93	In `PROGRESS.md`, next to each objective, record which blueprint chapter backs it. Example:
94	
95	```markdown
96	1. **`Algebra/WLocal.lean`** — resolve 3 sorries. Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Algebra_WLocal.tex` (theorems `thm:wLocal_iff`, `thm:wLocal_of_surjective`).
97	```
98	
99	The prover will read the chapter file mentioned here.
100	
101	## Long-arc Strategy
102	
103	`STRATEGY.md` is your living arc of how the project gets from its current state to "complete". `PROGRESS.md` scopes the next iteration; `STRATEGY.md` is the arc that contains every iteration. Only you write to it — the prover, refactor, and review agents never read or write it. The mathematician reads it, so keep it human-readable.
104	
105	Read it early in every iteration, before deciding which sorries to assign or whether to trigger a refactor, so the next iteration is grounded in the bigger picture.
106	
107	Update it after processing prover/review results and before writing `PROGRESS.md` or the blueprint. Describe the *current* plan only: the remaining steps from today's state to the end-state, in roughly the order they need to happen, with a rough effort estimate (iterations, LOC) per step. Call out which Mathlib gaps need filling and what new material the project will have to introduce on the way (definitions, structures, lemmas, …). Do not narrate past iterations — the Revision log captures history.
108	
109	Aim for the big picture, not the details. Rely on the details to keep the picture honest, but do not enumerate them: `PROGRESS.md` and the blueprint hold the specifics. The mathematician should be able to read the strategy.
110	
111	If nothing strategically changed this iteration, leave the body alone and add nothing to the Revision log. When the strategy changes, rewrite the affected parts in place and append one bullet to the Revision log explaining *why*: `- iter NNN — <one-line reason>`.
112	
113	## Feasibility Gate
114	
115	When facing difficult tasks, you and your agents should always try to think harder and should never delegate the task to other iterations or other agents. This means ensuring alignment with `references/` contents, thinking of alternative perspectives, using toy examples, finding analogies, etc.
116	
117	You should always question your previous work. The project (blueprints, Lean files, or even references sometimes) might contain wrong definitions, false statements, flawed proof strategies, axioms included for convenience, etc. If you identify such a critical issue, whether new or present since many iterations, you should absolutely address it, for instance by triggering a refactor.
118	
119	You should also be resilient when encountering obstacles and consider whether `Mathlib` contains the necessary infrastructure to solve the problem, or whether the current strategy requires filling its gaps. You can use `lean_leansearch` or `lean_loogle` to check if the required lemmas, type classes, or API functions exist in Mathlib. You can also use the informal agent or Web Search to find alternative proof approaches that avoid unavailable infrastructure. If alternative approaches significantly increase the chances of success, you may consider calling the refactor agent. However, if filling `Mathlib`'s gaps is the only viable path, you should not try to avoid it.
120	
121	## Triggering a Refactor
122	
123	While triggering a refactor should always be **strongly** motivated both mathematically and practically, it might sometimes be necessary. In this case:
124	
125	1. Write the necessary changes in the blueprint. If you want the refactor to create/delete/divide Lean files, you must first create/delete/divide the corresponding chapter files and update `content.tex` accordingly; the refactor agent will then update the Lean files to match the new blueprint structure. If you want the refactor to change definitions, signatures, types, or imports, you should also ensure that the blueprint reflects the desired content.
126	2. Write a directive to `.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`. It should include a clear and strongly justified description of the problem, a detailed mathematical justification for the proposed changes, and a precise list of the changes you want the refactor agent to make, along with references to the relevant blueprint chapters.
127	3. The refactor agent will execute your directive in the next phase. The refactor agent can change definitions, signatures, types, imports, and module structure, and can create/delete Lean files, as long as this doesn't conflict with `archon-protected.yaml`, but it cannot fill proofs. Broken proofs become `sorry`.
128	4. After the refactor completes, you will be re-invoked to verify the result and set objectives for the provers.
129	
130	**Before writing the directive:**
131	1. Use `lean_leansearch` / `lean_loogle` to verify the target definitions are compatible with Mathlib.
132	2. If the mathematical justification is non-trivial, use the informal agent or Web Search to develop it first.
133	
134	**When to trigger a refactor?**
135	- If some Lean files become too large and could be decomposed into semantically meaningful modules. If some proofs are too long and could be decomposed into smaller parts (Lemmas, etc.). In that case, you should ensure that blueprint chapters are decomposed accordingly and trigger a refactor to split the corresponding Lean files.
136	- If the proof strategy requires structural changes to the definitions, types, or module structure (e.g. modifying signatures, moving definitions, deleting or renaming a statement, etc.) that don't conflict with `archon-protected.yaml`. In that case, you should update the blueprint to reflect the desired structure and trigger a refactor to implement it.
137	
138	## Post-Refactor Verification
139	
140	After the refactor agent runs, you will be re-invoked. When `task_results/refactor.md` exists, you are in a **post-refactor verification pass**. In this pass:
141	
142	1. **Read `task_results/refactor.md` first.** Understand what the refactor agent changed, what new sorries were introduced, and whether compilation succeeded.
143	2. **Verify the changes match your directive:**
144	   - Check that definitions were changed as requested (read the affected `.lean` files)
145	   - Check compilation of affected files with `lean_diagnostic_messages`
146	   - If the refactor agent reported problems or partial completion, document them in `task_pending.md`
147	3. **Do NOT write another `REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` in this pass.** The loop only runs one refactor per iteration.
148	4. **Set prover objectives:** Update `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the provers.
149	5. **Update `task_pending.md`:** Record the refactor as context.
150	
151	## Providing Informal Content to the Prover
152	
153	The prover performs significantly better when given rich informal mathematical guidance. Before assigning a task, you must ensure the prover has access to the relevant informal proof or proof sketch.
154	
155	**How to provide informal content:**
156	
157	- **Short hints** (a few sentences): Write directly in `PROGRESS.md` under the task objectives. Example: "Key idea: use Bolzano-Weierstrass to extract a convergent subsequence, then show the limit satisfies the property."
158	
159	- **Medium content** (a paragraph or two): Write as comments in the corresponding `.lean` file, above the declaration with `sorry`. Use `/- ... -/` block comments.
160	
161	- **Long content** (a full proof sketch, paper summary, or multi-step construction): Write in the relevant chapter `.tex` file in the blueprint. Reference the blueprint chapter in `PROGRESS.md` next to the objective.
162	
163	- **Other possibilities**: The above methods should be prioritized, but if relevant, you may use Web Search to find new references, you may also write a separate markdown file in the project (e.g. `informal_sketches/some_lemma.md`) and link to it from `PROGRESS.md`. 
164	
165	**No matter which method you choose, always record in `PROGRESS.md`** where the informal content is located, so the prover can obtain it without searching.
166	
167	**When the reference is vague** (e.g., "by Hiblot 1975" without proof details):
168	1. Use `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` to generate an informal proof sketch from an external model
169	2. Use Web Search to find the referenced paper and extract the key proof steps
170	3. Write the result into a file and record the path in `PROGRESS.md`
171	4. Do this **before** assigning the task to the prover — don't send the prover in blind
172	
173	## Recognizing Prover Failure Modes
174	
175	### "Mathlib doesn't have it" — Missing Infrastructure
176	The #1 failure mode. The prover reports that a sorry is unfillable because Mathlib lacks the infrastructure, then stops.
177	
178	**Your response:** This is YOUR job to solve, not the prover's. Never just pass it back with "try harder." You must actively find an alternative proof route:
179	
180	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`) — ask it: "Prove X without using [the missing infrastructure]. Only use tools available in Lean 4 Mathlib." Get a concrete alternative proof sketch.
181	2. **Use Web Search** — find the referenced paper or alternative proofs of the same result that avoid the missing infrastructure.
182	3. **Decompose differently** — break the problem into sub-lemmas where each sub-lemma only needs available infrastructure. The prover can implement Mathlib-level lemmas if you give it clear, self-contained goals.
183	4. **Check `mathlib-unavailable-theorems.md`** — if the missing infrastructure is in a known-unavailable domain, don't waste time looking for it. Focus on detours.
184	5. **If the infrastructure gap is in the definition itself** — trigger a refactor to change the definition so it doesn't require the missing infrastructure downstream.
185	
186	Write the re-routed informal proof into the corresponding chapter `.tex` file (as a `\begin{proof} ... \end{proof}` body), then reassign the task to the prover. Do not reassign without providing an alternative in the chapter.
187	
188	### Wrong Construction — Building on a Flawed Foundation
189	The prover chose a wrong construction and the sorry is mathematically unfillable, but the prover keeps trying instead of backtracking.
190	
191	**Your response:** If the fix is within a single file, instruct the prover to revert. If the fix requires cross-file changes, trigger a refactor. Update the chapter `.tex` with the correct construction before the next prover round.
192	
193	### Not Using Web Search
194	The prover searches only within Mathlib and gives up when it finds nothing, even when the blueprint references a specific paper.
195	
196	**Your response:** Explicitly instruct: "Use Web Search to find [paper name/arXiv ID], read the proof, decompose it into sub-lemmas, and formalize step by step." Update the chapter with the retrieved proof sketch.
197	
198	### Early Stopping on Hard Problems
199	The prover stops and reports "done" when the remaining sorry requires significant effort.
200	
201	**Your response:** Reject the report. Break the hard problem into smaller sub-goals in the chapter `.tex` and assign them one at a time. Frame it as: "Formalize just sub-lemma L1 from the blueprint, then report back."
202	
203	### Using tricks (e.g. axioms, ad-hoc definitions, weakening hypotheses) to bypass hard parts
204	The prover introduces new axioms or definitions that aren't in the blueprint to fill sorries, then reports completion. You should also never propose such tricks as a plan agent.
205	
206	**Your response:** Reject the report. Such tricks should not be accepted; they should be documented and then removed. You should then try to understand why this route was chosen and ensure that it will not be reproduced.
207	
208	## Assessing Prover Progress
209	
210	### Three Indicators
211	| Indicator | Meaning |
212	|-----------|---------|
213	| Sorry count (decreasing) | Direct progress — a sorry was filled |
214	| Code line count (increasing) | Infrastructure building — helpers, definitions |
215	| `\leanok` marks added | Prover confirmed formalization against the blueprint |
216	
217	Line count increasing + sorry count unchanged = the prover is building infrastructure. This is real progress.
218	Line count unchanged + sorry count unchanged = zero progress.
219	
220	### Deep Stuck vs Early Abandonment
221	| Pattern | Diagnosis | Response |
222	|---------|-----------|----------|
223	| 800+ lines, 2-3 sorries left | Deep stuck — needs math hint or infrastructure | Provide informal guidance via informal_agent, suggest specific decomposition |
224	| <200 lines, sorry remaining | Early abandonment — prover gave up too quickly | Push harder: break into sub-goals, provide richer informal content |
225	
226	## Verification
227	
228	After a prover reports completion, always verify independently:
229	1. Check sorry count: `${LEAN4_PYTHON_BIN:-python3} "$LEAN4_SCRIPTS/sorry_analyzer.py" <file> --format=summary`
230	2. Check compilation: `lean_diagnostic_messages(file)` or `lake env lean <file>`
231	3. Check axioms: no new `axiom` declarations
232	4. Check blueprint consistency: `leanblueprint checkdecls` flags Lean names in the blueprint that don't exist. Run this after the prover has renamed or removed declarations.
233	
234	Never advance to the next stage based solely on the prover's word.
235	
236	## Dependency graph
237	
238	Before scoping objectives, run the bundled dependency-graph script
239	instead of reconstructing the dependency map by hand. It parses every
240	`.lean` file's imports plus every `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` for
241	`\lean{…}` / `\uses{…}` / `\proves{…}` / `\leanok` / `\notready`, and
242	emits a JSON view of the whole project in well under a second:
243	
244	```
245	${LEAN4_PYTHON_BIN:-python3} "$LEAN4_SCRIPTS/dependency_graph.py" . --format=json
246	```
247	
248	Use `--format=summary` for a one-screen overview, `--format=dot` if
249	you need to share the graph elsewhere. Read this once per iteration to
250	decide objective ordering — files with no upstream sorries should be
251	formalised first, downstream files later.
252	
253	## Decomposition Strategy
254	
255	When a prover is stuck on a large theorem:
256	1. Read the blueprint chapter to identify sub-lemma structure (L1, L2, L3, ...)
257	2. Read the files in `References/` related to it, if any, to ensure you understand and align with the original proof.
258	3. Check if the chapter is detailed enough — if not, expand it first (using informal_agent / Web Search)
259	4. Assign one sub-lemma at a time: "Fill sorry for L1 only"
260	5. After L1 is done, verify, then assign L2
261	6. Record each sub-lemma's status in `PROGRESS.md`
262	
263	## Context Management
264	
265	Each prover iteration starts with fresh context. The prover does not remember previous iterations.
266	
267	- Provide **self-contained** objectives in `PROGRESS.md` — include all context the prover needs
268	- Point the prover at its blueprint chapter — that is where the mathematical content lives
269	- When a prover gets stuck on the same failure across multiple iterations, it is re-discovering the same dead end. Change the approach entirely — do not just repeat "try again"
270	- Document dead ends in `PROGRESS.md` so the prover doesn't repeat them
271	
272	## Multi-Agent Coordination
273	
274	Provers run in parallel — one agent per file. Your objectives must be structured accordingly.
275	
276	### Writing objectives
277	
278	Number each objective clearly (1, 2, 3, ...). Each objective maps to **exactly one file**. Never assign two objectives to the same file. Reference the blueprint chapter alongside.
279	
280	```markdown

8	- `Jacobian C : Over (Spec (.of k))` = the connected component of the identity in the Picard scheme of `C`, equipped with `GrpObj`, `IsProper`, `GeometricallyIrreducible`, and `SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C)`.

11	## Honest assessment of feasibility (Mathlib b80f227, Lean 4.30.0-rc2)
12	
13	A full Mathlib survey (see `task_pending.md`) shows that **every single one of the nine protected declarations is blocked by non-trivial missing Mathlib infrastructure** — the gap is not in the proofs, it is in the surrounding theory. In particular:

16	|---|---|

public import Lean.Elab.MutualInductive

namespace Lean.Elab.Command

open Meta

private def ensureValidNamespace (name : Name) : MacroM Unit := do
  match name with
  | .str p s =>
    if s == "_root_" then
      Macro.throwError s!"invalid namespace `{name}`, `_root_` is a reserved namespace"
    ensureValidNamespace p
  | .num .. => Macro.throwError s!"invalid namespace `{name}`, it must not contain numeric parts"
  | .anonymous => return ()

private def setDeclIdName (declId : Syntax) (nameNew : Name) : Syntax :=
  let (id, _) := expandDeclIdCore declId
  -- We should not update the name of `def _root_.` declarations
  assert! !(`_root_).isPrefixOf id
  let idStx := mkIdent nameNew |>.raw.setInfo declId.getHeadInfo
  if declId.isIdent then
    idStx
  else
    declId.setArg 0 idStx

/-- Return `true` if `stx` is a `Command.declaration`, and it is a declaration that always has a name. -/
private def isNamedDecl (stx : Syntax) : Bool :=
  if !stx.isOfKind ``Lean.Parser.Command.declaration then
    false
  else
    let decl := stx[1]
    let k := decl.getKind
    k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.abbrev ||
    k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.definition ||
    k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.theorem ||
    k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.opaque ||
    k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.axiom ||
    k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.inductive ||
    k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.classInductive ||
    k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.structure

/-- Return `true` if `stx` is an `instance` declaration command -/
private def isInstanceDecl (stx : Syntax) : Bool :=
  stx.isOfKind ``Lean.Parser.Command.declaration &&
  stx[1].getKind == ``Lean.Parser.Command.instance

/-- Return `some name` if `stx` is a declaration named `name` -/
private def getDeclName? (stx : Syntax) : Option Name := do
  if isNamedDecl stx then
    let (id, _) := expandDeclIdCore stx[1][1]
    some id
  else if isInstanceDecl stx then
    let optDeclId := stx[1][3]
    if optDeclId.isNone then none
    else
      let (id, _) := expandDeclIdCore optDeclId[0]
      some id
  else
    none

/--
Update the name of the given declaration.
This function assumes `stx` is not a nameless instance.
-/
private def setDeclName (stx : Syntax) (name : Name) : Syntax :=
  if isNamedDecl stx then
    stx.setArg 1 <| stx[1].setArg 1 <| setDeclIdName stx[1][1] name
  else if isInstanceDecl stx then
    -- We never set the name of nameless instance declarations
    assert! !stx[1][3].isNone
    stx.setArg 1 <| stx[1].setArg 3 <| stx[1][3].setArg 0 <| setDeclIdName stx[1][3][0] name
  else
    stx

/--
  Given declarations such as `@[...] def Foo.Bla.f ...` return `some (Foo.Bla, @[...] def f ...)`
  Remark: if the id starts with `_root_`, we return `none`.
-/
private def expandDeclNamespace? (stx : Syntax) : MacroM (Option (Name × Syntax)) := do
  let some name := getDeclName? stx | return none
  if (`_root_).isPrefixOf name then
    ensureValidNamespace (name.replacePrefix `_root_ Name.anonymous)
    return none
  let scpView := extractMacroScopes name
  match scpView.name with
  | .str .anonymous _ => return none
  | .str pre shortName => return some (pre, setDeclName stx { scpView with name := .mkSimple shortName }.review)
  | _ => return none

def elabAxiom (modifiers : Modifiers) (stx : Syntax) : CommandElabM Unit := do
  -- leading_parser "axiom " >> declId >> declSig
  let declId             := stx[1]
  let (binders, typeStx) := expandDeclSig stx[2]
  runTermElabM fun vars => do
    let scopeLevelNames ← Term.getLevelNames
    let ⟨shortName, declName, allUserLevelNames, docString?⟩ ← Term.expandDeclId (← getCurrNamespace) scopeLevelNames declId modifiers
    addDeclarationRangesForBuiltin declName modifiers.stx stx
    Term.withAutoBoundImplicit do
    Term.withAutoBoundImplicitForbiddenPred (fun n => shortName == n) do
    Term.withDeclName declName <| Term.withLevelNames allUserLevelNames <| Term.elabBinders binders.getArgs fun xs => do
      Term.applyAttributesAt declName modifiers.attrs AttributeApplicationTime.beforeElaboration
      let type ← Term.elabType typeStx
      Term.synthesizeSyntheticMVarsNoPostponing
      let xs ← Term.addAutoBoundImplicits xs (declId.getTailPos? (canonicalOnly := true))
      let type ← instantiateMVars type
      let type ← mkForallFVars xs type
      let type ← mkForallFVars vars type (usedOnly := true)
      let type ← Term.levelMVarToParam type
      let usedParams  := collectLevelParams {} type |>.params
      match sortDeclLevelParams scopeLevelNames allUserLevelNames usedParams with
      | Except.error msg      => throwErrorAt stx msg
      | Except.ok levelParams =>
        let type ← instantiateMVars type
        let decl := Declaration.axiomDecl {
          name        := declName,
          levelParams := levelParams,
          type        := type,
          isUnsafe    := modifiers.isUnsafe
        }
        trace[Elab.axiom] "{declName} : {type}"
        Term.ensureNoUnassignedMVars decl
        addDecl decl

        Term.applyAttributesAt declName modifiers.attrs AttributeApplicationTime.afterTypeChecking
        if isExtern (← getEnv) declName then
          compileDecl decl
        if let some (doc, isVerso) := docString? then
          addDocStringOf isVerso declName binders doc
        Term.applyAttributesAt declName modifiers.attrs AttributeApplicationTime.afterCompilation
        withSaveInfoContext do  -- save new env with docstring and decl
          Term.addTermInfo' declId (← mkConstWithLevelParams declName) (isBinder := true)
        enableRealizationsForConst declName
open Lean.Parser.Command.InternalSyntax in
/--
Macro that expands a declaration with a complex name into an explicit `namespace` block.
Implementing this step as a macro means that reuse checking is handled by `elabCommand`.
 -/
@[builtin_macro Lean.Parser.Command.declaration]
def expandNamespacedDeclaration : Macro := fun stx => do
  match (← expandDeclNamespace? stx) with
  | some (ns, newStx) => do
    -- Limit ref variability for incrementality; see Note [Incremental Macros]
    let declTk := stx[1][0]
    let ns := mkIdentFrom declTk ns
    withRef declTk `(namespace $ns $endLocalScopeSyntax:command $(⟨newStx⟩) end $ns)
  | none => Macro.throwUnsupported

@[builtin_command_elab declaration, builtin_incremental]
def elabDeclaration : CommandElab := fun stx => do
  withExporting (isExporting := (← getScope).isPublic) do
  let modifiers : TSyntax ``Parser.Command.declModifiers := ⟨stx[0]⟩
  let decl     := stx[1]
  let declKind := decl.getKind
  if isDefLike decl then
    -- only case implementing incrementality currently
    elabMutualDef #[stx]
  else withoutCommandIncrementality true do
    -- use hash of declaration name, if any, as stable quot context; `elabMutualDef` has its own
    -- handling
    withInitQuotContext (getDeclName? stx |>.map hash) do
    let mut modifiers ← elabModifiers modifiers
    if (← getScope).isMeta && modifiers.computeKind == .regular then
      modifiers := { modifiers with computeKind := .meta }
    withExporting (isExporting := modifiers.isInferredPublic (← getEnv)) do
      if declKind == ``Lean.Parser.Command.«axiom» then
        elabAxiom modifiers decl
      else if declKind == ``Lean.Parser.Command.«inductive»
          || declKind == ``Lean.Parser.Command.«coinductive»
          || declKind == ``Lean.Parser.Command.classInductive
          || declKind == ``Lean.Parser.Command.«structure» then
        elabInductive modifiers decl
      else
        throwError "unexpected declaration"

/-- Return true if all elements of the mutual-block are definitions/theorems/abbrevs. -/
private def isMutualDefLike (stx : Syntax) : Bool :=
  stx[1].getArgs.all fun elem =>
    let decl := elem[1]
    isDefLike decl

private def isMutualPreambleCommand (stx : Syntax) : Bool :=
  let k := stx.getKind
  k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.variable ||
  k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.universe ||
  k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.check ||
  k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.set_option ||
  k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.open

private partial def splitMutualPreamble (elems : Array Syntax) : Option (Array Syntax × Array Syntax) :=
  let rec loop (i : Nat) : Option (Array Syntax × Array Syntax) :=
    if h : i < elems.size then
      if isMutualPreambleCommand elems[i] then
        loop (i+1)
      else if i == 0 then
        none -- `mutual` block does not contain any preamble commands
      else
        some (elems[*...i], elems[i...elems.size])
    else
      none -- a `mutual` block containing only preamble commands is not a valid `mutual` block
  loop 0

/--
Find the common namespace for the given names.
Example:
```
findCommonPrefix [`Lean.Elab.eval, `Lean.mkConst, `Lean.Elab.Tactic.evalTactic]
-- `Lean
```
-/
def findCommonPrefix (ns : List Name) : Name :=
  match ns with
  | [] => .anonymous
  | n :: ns => go n ns
where
  go (n : Name) (ns : List Name) : Name :=
    match n with
    | .anonymous => .anonymous
    | _ => match ns with
      | [] => n
      | n' :: ns => go (findCommon n.components n'.components) ns
  findCommon (as bs : List Name) : Name :=
    match as, bs with
    | a :: as, b :: bs => if a == b then a ++ findCommon as bs else .anonymous
    | _, _ => .anonymous


@[builtin_macro Lean.Parser.Command.mutual]
def expandMutualNamespace : Macro := fun stx => do
  let mut nss := #[]
  for elem in stx[1].getArgs do
    match (← expandDeclNamespace? elem) with
    | none        => Macro.throwUnsupported
    | some (n, _) => nss := nss.push n
  let common := findCommonPrefix nss.toList
  if common.isAnonymous then Macro.throwUnsupported
  let elemsNew ← stx[1].getArgs.mapM fun elem => do
    let some name := getDeclName? elem | unreachable!
    let view := extractMacroScopes name
    let nameNew := { view with name := view.name.replacePrefix common .anonymous }.review
    return setDeclName elem nameNew
  let ns := mkIdentFrom stx common
  let stxNew := stx.setArg 1 (mkNullNode elemsNew)
  `(namespace $ns $(⟨stxNew⟩) end $ns)

@[builtin_macro Lean.Parser.Command.mutual]
def expandMutualElement : Macro := fun stx => do
  let mut elemsNew := #[]
  let mut modified := false
  for elem in stx[1].getArgs do
    -- Don't trigger the `expandNamespacedDecl` macro, the namespace is handled by the mutual def
    -- elaborator directly instead
    if !elem.isOfKind ``Parser.Command.declaration then
      if let some elemNew ← expandMacro? elem then
        if elemNew.isOfKind nullKind then
          elemsNew := elemsNew ++ elemNew.getArgs
        else
          elemsNew := elemsNew.push elemNew
        modified := true
        continue
    elemsNew := elemsNew.push elem
  if modified then
    return stx.setArg 1 (mkNullNode elemsNew)
  else
    Macro.throwUnsupported

@[builtin_macro Lean.Parser.Command.mutual]
def expandMutualPreamble : Macro := fun stx =>
  match splitMutualPreamble stx[1].getArgs with
  | none => Macro.throwUnsupported
  | some (preamble, rest) => do
    let secCmd    ← `(section)
    let newMutual := stx.setArg 1 (mkNullNode rest)
    let endCmd    ← `(end)
    return mkNullNode (#[secCmd] ++ preamble ++ #[newMutual] ++ #[endCmd])

@[builtin_command_elab «mutual», builtin_incremental]
def elabMutual : CommandElab := fun stx => do
  withExporting (isExporting := (← getScope).isPublic) do
  if isMutualDefLike stx then
    -- only case implementing incrementality currently
    elabMutualDef stx[1].getArgs
  else withoutCommandIncrementality true do
    if ← isMutualInductive stx then
      elabMutualInductive stx[1].getArgs
    else
      throwError "invalid mutual block: either all elements of the block must be inductive/structure declarations, or they must all be definitions/theorems/abbrevs"

/- leading_parser "attribute " >> "[" >> sepBy1 (eraseAttr <|> Term.attrInstance) ", " >> "]" >> many1 ident -/
@[builtin_command_elab «attribute»] def elabAttr : CommandElab := fun stx => do
  let mut attrInsts := #[]
  let mut toErase := #[]
  for attrKindStx in stx[2].getSepArgs do
    if attrKindStx.getKind == ``Lean.Parser.Command.eraseAttr then
      let attrName := attrKindStx[1].getId.eraseMacroScopes
      if isAttribute (← getEnv) attrName then
        toErase := toErase.push attrName
      else
        logErrorAt attrKindStx m!"unknown attribute [{attrName}]"
    else
      attrInsts := attrInsts.push attrKindStx
  let attrs ← elabAttrs attrInsts
  let idents := stx[4].getArgs
  for ident in idents do withRef ident <| liftTermElabM do
    /-
    HACK to allow `attribute` command to disable builtin simprocs.
    TODO: find a better solution. Example: have some "fake" declaration
    for builtin simprocs.
    -/
    let declNames ←
      try
        realizeGlobalConstWithInfos ident
      catch _ =>
        let name := ident.getId.eraseMacroScopes
        if (← Simp.isBuiltinSimproc name) then
          pure [name]
        else
          throwUnknownConstantAt ident name
    let declName ← ensureNonAmbiguous ident declNames
    withExporting (isExporting := !isPrivateName declName && attrs.any (·.kind != .local)) do
      recordExtraModUseFromDecl (isMeta := false) declName
    Term.applyAttributes declName attrs
    for attrName in toErase do
      Attribute.erase declName attrName
    if (← getEnv).isImportedConst declName then
      if let some attr := attrs.find? (·.kind != .local) then
        -- If an imported declaration is marked with a non-local attribute, Shake must be informed
        -- about this indirect reference
        recordIndirectModUse attr.name.toString declName

@[builtin_command_elab Lean.Parser.Command.«initialize»] def elabInitialize : CommandElab
  | stx@`($declModifiers:declModifiers $kw:initializeKeyword $[$id? : $type? ←]? $doSeq) => do
    let attrId := mkIdentFrom stx <| if kw.raw[0].isToken "initialize" then `init else `builtin_init
    if let (some id, some type) := (id?, type?) then
      let `(Parser.Command.declModifiersT| $[$doc?:docComment]? $[@[$attrs?,*]]? $(vis?)? $[meta%$meta?]? $[unsafe%$unsafe?]?) := stx[0]
        | throwErrorAt declModifiers "invalid initialization command, unexpected modifiers"
      let defStx ← `($[$doc?:docComment]? @[$attrId:ident initFn, $(attrs?.getD ∅),*] $(vis?)? $[meta%$meta?]? opaque $id : $type)
      let mut fullId := (← getCurrNamespace) ++ id.getId
      if vis?.any (·.raw.isOfKind ``Parser.Command.private) then
        fullId := mkPrivateName (← getEnv) fullId
      -- We need to add `id`'s ranges *before* elaborating `initFn` (and then `id` itself) as
      -- otherwise the info context created by `with_decl_name` will be incomplete and break the
      -- call hierarchy
      addDeclarationRangesForBuiltin fullId ⟨defStx.raw[0]⟩ defStx.raw[1]
      let vis := Parser.Command.visibility.ofBool (!isPrivateName fullId)
      elabCommand (← `(
        $vis:visibility $[meta%$meta?]? $[unsafe%$unsafe?]? def initFn : IO $type := with_decl_name% $(mkIdent fullId) do $doSeq
        $defStx:command))
    else
      let `(Parser.Command.declModifiersT| $[$doc?:docComment]? $[@[$attrs?,*]]? $(_)? $[meta%$meta?]? $[unsafe%$unsafe?]?) := declModifiers
        | throwErrorAt declModifiers "invalid initialization command, unexpected modifiers"
      let attrs := (attrs?.map (·.getElems)).getD #[]
      let attrs := attrs.push (← `(Lean.Parser.Term.attrInstance| $attrId:ident))
      -- `[builtin_init]` can be private as it is used for local codegen only but `[init]` must be
      -- available for the interpreter.
      let vis := Parser.Command.visibility.ofBool (attrId.getId == `init)
      elabCommand (← `($[$doc?:docComment]? @[$[$attrs],*] $vis:visibility $[meta%$meta?]? $[unsafe%$unsafe?]? def initFn : IO Unit := do $doSeq))
  | _ => throwUnsupportedSyntax

builtin_initialize
  registerTraceClass `Elab.axiom

end Lean.Elab.Command
"}

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.Grp.Abelian
9	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.Grp.Adjunctions
10	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.DerivedCategory.Ext.Basic

16	/-!
17	# Sheaf cohomology
18	
19	Let `C` be a category equipped with a Grothendieck topology `J`.
20	We define the cohomology types `Sheaf.H F n` of an abelian
21	sheaf `F` on the site `(C, J)` for all `n : ℕ`. These abelian
22	groups are defined as the `Ext`-groups from the constant abelian
23	sheaf with values `ℤ` (actually `ULift ℤ`) to `F`.
24	
25	We also define `Sheaf.cohomologyPresheaf F n : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat`
26	which is the presheaf which sends `U` to the `n`th `Ext`-group
27	from the free abelian sheaf generated by the presheaf
28	of sets `yoneda.obj U` to `F`.
29	
30	## TODO
31	* if `U` is a terminal object of `C`, define an isomorphism
32	  `(F.cohomologyPresheaf n).obj (Opposite.op U) ≃+ Sheaf.H F n`.
33	* if `U : C`, define an isomorphism
34	  `(F.cohomologyPresheaf n).obj (Opposite.op U) ≃+ Sheaf.H (F.over U) n`.
35	
36	-/
37	
38	@[expose] public noncomputable section
39	
40	assert_not_exists TwoSidedIdeal
41	
42	universe w' w v u
43	
44	namespace CategoryTheory
45	
46	open Abelian
47	
48	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
49	
50	namespace Sheaf
51	
52	section
53	
54	variable (F : Sheaf J AddCommGrpCat.{w})
55	  [HasSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{w}] [HasExt.{w'} (Sheaf J AddCommGrpCat.{w})]
56	
57	/-- The cohomology of an abelian sheaf in degree `n`. -/
58	def H (n : ℕ) : Type w' :=
59	  Ext ((constantSheaf J AddCommGrpCat.{w}).obj (AddCommGrpCat.of (ULift ℤ))) F n
60	deriving AddCommGroup
61	
62	variable (J) in
63	/-- Cohomology of an abelian sheaf in degree `n` as a functor. -/
64	def cohomologyFunctor (n : ℕ) :
65	    Sheaf J AddCommGrpCat.{w} ⥤ AddCommGrpCat.{w'} :=
66	  (extFunctor.{w'} n).obj <|
67	    .op <| ((constantSheaf J AddCommGrpCat.{w}).obj (AddCommGrpCat.of (ULift ℤ)))
68	deriving Functor.Additive
69	
70	lemma cohomologyFunctor_obj (n : ℕ) (F : Sheaf J AddCommGrpCat.{w}) :
71	    (cohomologyFunctor J n).obj F = F.H n :=
72	  rfl
73	
74	lemma subsingleton_H_of_isZero {F : Sheaf J AddCommGrpCat.{w}} (h : Limits.IsZero F) (n : ℕ) :
75	    Subsingleton (F.H n) := by
76	  rw [← cohomologyFunctor_obj]
77	  apply AddCommGrpCat.subsingleton_of_isZero
78	  exact Functor.map_isZero (cohomologyFunctor J n) h
79	
80	end
81	
82	section
83	
84	variable [HasSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{v}] [HasExt.{w'} (Sheaf J AddCommGrpCat.{v})]
85	
86	variable (J) in
87	/-- The bifunctor which sends an abelian sheaf `F` and an object `U` to the
88	`n`th Ext-group from the free abelian sheaf generated by the
89	presheaf of sets `yoneda.obj U` to `F`. -/
90	def cohomologyPresheafFunctor (n : ℕ) :
91	    Sheaf J AddCommGrpCat.{v} ⥤ Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat.{w'} :=
92	  Functor.flip
93	    (Functor.op (yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj
94	      AddCommGrpCat.free ⋙ presheafToSheaf _ _) ⋙ extFunctor n)
95	
96	/-- Given an abelian sheaf `F`, this is the presheaf which sends `U`
97	to the `n`th Ext-group from the free abelian sheaf generated by the
98	presheaf of sets `yoneda.obj U` to `F`. -/
99	abbrev cohomologyPresheaf (F : Sheaf J AddCommGrpCat.{v}) (n : ℕ) :
100	    Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat.{w'} :=
101	  (cohomologyPresheafFunctor J n).obj F
102	
103	/-- Given an abelian sheaf `F` on `(C, J)`, `n : ℕ` and `X : C`, this is
104	the degree-`n` sheaf cohomology of `X` with values in `F`. -/
105	abbrev H' (F : Sheaf J AddCommGrpCat.{v}) (n : ℕ) (X : C) :
106	    AddCommGrpCat.{w'} :=
107	  (F.cohomologyPresheaf n).obj (Opposite.op X)
108	
109	end
110	
111	end Sheaf
112	
113	end CategoryTheory

public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.Grp.Biproducts
public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.MayerVietorisSquare
public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.SheafCohomology.Basic

/-!
# The Mayer-Vietoris exact sequence in sheaf cohomology

Let `C` be a category equipped with a Grothendieck topology `J`.
Let `S : J.MayerVietorisSquare` be a Mayer-Vietoris square for `J`.
Let `F` be an abelian sheaf on `(C, J)`.

In this file, we obtain a long exact Mayer-Vietoris sequence:

`... ⟶ H^n(S.X₄, F) ⟶ H^n(S.X₂, F) ⊞ H^n(S.X₃, F) ⟶ H^n(S.X₁, F) ⟶ H^{n + 1}(S.X₄, F) ⟶ ...`

-/

@[expose] public section

universe w v u

namespace CategoryTheory

open Category Opposite Limits Abelian

variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
  [HasWeakSheafify J (Type v)] [HasSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{v}]
  [HasExt.{w} (Sheaf J AddCommGrpCat.{v})]

namespace GrothendieckTopology.MayerVietorisSquare

variable (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J AddCommGrpCat.{v})

/-- The sum of two restriction maps in sheaf cohomology. -/
noncomputable def toBiprod (n : ℕ) :
    F.H' n S.X₄ ⟶ F.H' n S.X₂ ⊞ F.H' n S.X₃ :=
  biprod.lift ((F.cohomologyPresheaf n).map S.f₂₄.op)
      ((F.cohomologyPresheaf n).map S.f₃₄.op)

lemma toBiprod_apply {n : ℕ} (y : F.H' n S.X₄) :
    S.toBiprod F n y = (AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).inv
      ⟨(F.cohomologyPresheaf n).map S.f₂₄.op y,
        (F.cohomologyPresheaf n).map S.f₃₄.op y⟩ := by
  apply (AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv.injective
  dsimp [toBiprod]
  ext
  · rw [Iso.addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv_apply,
      Iso.addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv_apply,
      ← AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd_inv_comp_fst_apply,
      Iso.hom_inv_id_apply, ← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply,
      biprod.lift_fst, Iso.inv_hom_id_apply]
  · rw [Iso.addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv_apply,

import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafModuleK
open CategoryTheory Limits Abelian

universe u v
namespace TestNS

-- Direct mirror of Mathlib's Sheaf.cohomologyPresheaf, but for the ModuleCat k flavor.
noncomputable def Scheme.HModule'_cohomologyPresheafFunctor
    (k : Type u) [Field k]
    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] (J : GrothendieckTopology C)
    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
    (n : ℕ) : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k) ⥤ Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat :=
  Functor.flip
    ((yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k) ⋙
      presheafToSheaf _ _).op ⋙ extFunctor n)

/-- Presheaf of cohomology groups (ModuleCat k flavor): X ↦ HModule' k F n X. -/
noncomputable abbrev Scheme.HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf
    (k : Type u) [Field k]
    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat :=
  (Scheme.HModule'_cohomologyPresheafFunctor k J n).obj F

-- Identification at op X: the presheaf evaluates to HModule' k F n X.
example {k : Type u} [Field k]
    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) (X : C) :
    ((Scheme.HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op X) : Type u)
      = AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule' k F n X := rfl

end TestNS

138	## Forbidden shortcuts (documented once)
139	
140	- `genus C := 0` or any constant — false in genus $\ge 1$.
141	- `Jacobian C := 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k)))` (terminal scheme) — forces `genus = 0` to discharge `smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus`, which is mathematically wrong.
142	- `Jacobian C := 𝔸¹_k`, `Spec L` (any constant scheme) — same obstruction.
143	- `ofCurve P := toUnit C ≫ η[Jacobian C]` (constant map to identity) — falsifies `exists_unique_ofCurve_comp` whenever a non-constant pointed morphism $C \to A$ exists (i.e. for any $g(C) \ge 1$).
144	- `LineBundle X := Unit` / `PUnit` / any vacuous placeholder — destroys the downstream Picard chain.
145	- New `axiom` declarations — forbidden by plan-agent rules.
146	- `PicardFunctor C := Discrete PUnit` or any vacuous functor — destroys representability and the downstream Jacobian chain.
147	- Closing `PicardFunctor.representable` on top of the global-sections-approximate `LineBundle` — silently asserts representability of the wrong functor. Keep the sorry in place.
148	- Symmetric topological-equality form of rigidity (`∀ x ∈ U, g₁.left.base x = g₂.left.base x ⇒ g₁ = g₂`) — mathematically false in characteristic `p` (Frobenius). Use the scheme-level form (`U.ι ≫ g₁.left = U.ι ≫ g₂.left ⇒ g₁ = g₂`) as in the iter-002 closure.
149	- Vacuous-presheaf / `Discrete PUnit`-valued / `PUnit.{u+1}`-valued sheaf shortcuts on Phase A scaffolding — destroys the downstream cohomology chain; rejected on sight.
150	- (Resolved iter-006) On `Scheme.toModuleKSheaf` and helpers: substituting any helper with a vacuous closure (e.g. `algebraSection` returning `Algebra.id`, `kToSection` returning the zero ring map, `toModuleKPresheaf` skipping the structure-morphism algebra map and using the trivial `k`-action discarding `C.hom`) was explicitly forbidden because the resulting `Module k` instance on `(toModuleKSheaf C).H 1` would be the zero one, falsifying `genus`. The iter-006 prover round closed all eight helpers honestly using the actual structure morphism `C.hom`, with only three minor mathematically-equivalent deviations from the recorded bodies (drop `.symm`; trim simp set; drop `Functor.ext` tail and use `Sheaf.property`).
151	- For the iter-007 polish track: closing the `toModuleKSheaf_forgetCompare` natural iso with a non-natural iso between unrelated functors is forbidden. The two sheaves agree on the nose at the underlying-presheaf level (probe-confirmed); the closure must be `Iso.refl _` (or an equivalent definitional-identity construction). The simp lemmas `toModuleKPresheaf_obj` and `PicardFunctorAb_forget_obj` are `rfl`-companions; closing them by anything other than `rfl` (or a defeq) is a flag.
152	- For the iter-010 H⁰ bridge: closing `HModule_zero_linearEquiv` with anything other than `Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀` (or a mathematically-equivalent rebuild) is a flag. Specifically, do NOT scaffold a polish-shape `HModule_forget` (`forget₂ HModule ↔ Sheaf.H ((sheafCompose _ (forget₂)).obj F)`) — Mathlib's `Sheaf.H` is `Ext ((constantSheaf _).obj (ULift ℤ)) F n` and `HModule` is `Ext ((constantSheaf _).obj (ModuleCat.of k k)) F n`; the units differ and `forget₂` does not produce the equality (iter-010 plan-agent finding, supersedes the over-optimistic session-15 `recommendations.md` proposal).

352	**Status: PLAN-AGENT PASS COMPLETE THIS ITERATION; REFACTOR + PROVER ROUNDS PENDING.** With iter-014's `HModule'` carrier in scope, iter-015 lays the natural follow-on companion: the degree-zero linear equivalence identifying `HModule' k F 0 X` with the corresponding Hom group from the `presheafToSheaf`-of-`yoneda ⋙ ModuleCat.free k` representable. This mirrors iter-010's `HModule_zero_linearEquiv` for the iter-014 `HModule'`, completing the algebraic-preliminary picture for Mayer-Vietoris LES setup (queued iter-016+).
